◆ Strategic Proposal — UK & Europe · 2026

ORN Connect

A Destination Management Company

One entity. One brand. ORN Connect is the UK and European Destination Management Company for the ORN Global Group — the central commercial contracting authority, the inbound reservations operation (powered by Nexus), and the 96+ vehicle operating fleet (previously Skyjet), all under one brand. TripFactory and all global group offices are ORN Connect's primary clients.

UK & European Experiences
Alpine Discovery
Above the Clouds

PreviouslyORN Holidays + Skyjet
PlatformNexus — reservations & fulfilment
Fleet96+ vehicles (previously Skyjet)

Entity · one P&L
96+

5+
Global client offices
£0
New external funding
The consolidation: ORN Connect brings together ORN Holidays (commercial contracting), Skyjet (transport — 96+ fleet, ISO 27001), and a new Nexus-powered reservations function into one DMC entity. One brand for every global office, trade agent, and consumer booking UK and European travel.
What ORN Connect Does

Six service lines. One company.

ORN Connect handles the complete UK and European inbound supply chain — from contracting hotels and ground product, to operating the coach fleet, to processing every reservation. Everything a global office needs to sell UK and European travel is available through one ORN Connect relationship.

Central Contracting Authority

ORN Connect holds all hotel, ground, experience, and attraction contracts for UK and Europe. No group office contracts independently. All rates maintained centrally by Ken Newton (European hotel portfolio — 27+ properties) and Manish Suchde (global DMC relationships). Distributed to all offices via Nexus platform or API feed.

Ken Newton · Manish Suchde · 27+ properties

Inbound DMC — All Channels

Official UK and European DMC for all inbound — TripFactory B2C, group offices selling outbound to Europe, inbound trade agents, and direct enquiries. FIT, GIT, MICE, escorted touring, and cruise all contracted, operated, and fulfilled by ORN Connect.

FIT · GIT · MICE · Escorted · Cruise

Transport Division — Previously Skyjet

ORN Connect operates the full 96+ vehicle fleet — 30+ touring coaches and 65+ Mercedes-Benz private transfer vehicles, previously operating as Skyjet. ISO 27001 certified. Adonis Iliopoulos leads transport. All departures and transfers now operate under ORN Connect brand.

Previously Skyjet · 96+ fleet · ISO 27001

Reservations — Powered by Nexus

All booking reservations, agent enquiries, group fulfilment, and pre-departure documentation handled through the Nexus platform — same system used by Nexus AU. Dedicated ORN Connect reservations agents at 17 Hanover Square. Head of Reservations is the priority hire.

Nexus platform · Res agents · Priority hire

Global Cruise Management

ORN Connect manages and distributes the ORN Cruise programme globally — contracting cruise lines, managing allotments, distributing to TAG and TripFactory. All cruise bookings processed by the ORN Connect Nexus reservations team at 17 Hanover Square.

ORN Cruise · Global allotments · Nexus

Outbound UK & Europe — Phase 4

From Phase 4 — once the inbound operation is profitable and fully staffed — ORN Connect extends to operate outbound holidays from the UK and Europe, using its contracted supplier base, the Nexus platform, and the in-house fleet. Not in initial scope.

Phase 4 only · Not initial scope
Global Client Network

TripFactory and the group — ORN Connect's clients

TripFactory and all ORN global offices are ORN Connect's primary clients. Each sells outbound from its location to UK and European inbound product — supplied, contracted, operated, and fulfilled by ORN Connect. Every booking generates income for ORN Connect. Every office has one point of contact: ORN Connect at 17 Hanover Square.

TripFactory — B2C Global Platform

TripFactory is ORN Connect's primary B2C distribution channel. ORN Connect series departures, inbound FIT packages, and cruise product are listed on TripFactory for direct consumer purchase globally. In Phase 4, TripFactory becomes the primary outbound channel for UK-originated product.

B2C · Global reach · Phase 4 outbound

Australia / New Zealand — Sahil Nijhawan

ANZ office sells outbound to Europe via ORN Connect. All UK and European product purchased through ORN Connect contracted rates via Nexus — the same system the ANZ team uses via Nexus AU. Transaction fee applies per booking. Yukti Kumar coordinates ANZ product requirements.

Sahil Nijhawan · Yukti Kumar · Nexus AU link

USA — Kirk Demeter / Kerryn Collins

USA office sells UK and European inbound product to the North American market. All bookings via ORN Connect Nexus platform. TAG B2B agent network managed by Kerryn Collins routes trade bookings directly through ORN Connect.

Kirk Demeter · Kerryn Collins · TAG B2B

Middle East — Baris Intepe (Gateway)

Turkey, Eastern Mediterranean, and Middle East inbound groups and FIT processed via ORN Connect. Baris Intepe manages the ME gateway ahead of ME CEO appointment. All ME-origin UK and European product contracted and fulfilled by ORN Connect.

Baris Intepe · Turkey · GCC · MENA

Incoming Europe — Eileen Corrales

Primary European ground partner. 27+ hotel properties across Scotland, England, and Wales contracted via Ken Newton. Provides in-country ground services for all ORN Connect touring departures. ORN Connect is the commercial authority; Incoming Europe delivers the ground.

Eileen Corrales · 27+ properties · Ken Newton

Inbound Trade Agents

UK-based inbound trade agents, overseas booking agents, and OTAs book UK and European product through ORN Connect via the Nexus agent portal — live rates, live availability, instant quote. All agent relationships managed by the ORN Connect reservations team.

Nexus agent portal · Trade · OTAs
Revenue Model

Five income streams — one P&L

As a single entity, all revenue flows to ORN Connect. Contracting margin, transport operations income, reservations commission, and management fees all sit on one P&L — making reporting cleaner and accountability clearer.

Transaction Fee
Per booking

Group office transaction fee

Applied to every booking by a group office using ORN Connect contracted rates — hotels, transfers, tours, cruise. Volume-linked — scales with group growth.

% Revenue Share
% of GTV

Operated departure participation

Percentage of gross travel value on every escorted tour departure and transfer operated by ORN Connect (previously Skyjet operations). One entity — one P&L line.

Inbound Commission
Net margin

Nexus inbound bookings

Net rate margin on all inbound bookings handled by ORN Connect's Nexus reservations team — FIT, GIT, groups, transfers, cruise.

Management Fee
Per office

Global client office retainer

Each group office (USA, AU/NZ, Middle East) pays a periodic management fee for access to ORN Connect contracted rates, the Nexus product hub, and API infrastructure.

Budget Reallocation
Consolidated

European office budget consolidation

European office budgets currently funding duplicate contracting (previously ORN Holidays) and transport (previously Skyjet) are consolidated into a single ORN Connect UK budget. No new net external funding required at launch. Nexus reservations team self-funds from inbound commission by Phase 2.

1
Entity · one P&L
5
Income streams
5+
Global client offices
£0
New external funding
Cost Model

One entity — no duplication

Consolidating ORN Holidays, Skyjet, and the reservations function under ORN Connect eliminates structural duplication. Funded entirely from reallocated existing budgets — no new spend required to launch.

Previous StructureOld StateORN Connect ModelImpact
ORN Holidays contractingSeparate commercial entity — fragmented from operationsORN Connect is the single commercial and contracting authoritySIMPLIFY
Skyjet transportSeparate brand — fleet and licences separate from DMC brandORN Connect Transport Division. Same fleet, same team — new brand. "Previously Skyjet" in transition commsREBRAND
No reservations functionBookings handled ad hoc — no dedicated UK inbound res teamORN Connect Nexus reservations team at 17 Hanover SquareESTABLISH
Duplicated EU contractingEach EU office sourcing own product — duplicated costORN Connect holds all EU contracts — all offices access via Nexus. One annual negotiationSAVE
Fragmented tour opsNo central departure programme or seat managementORN Connect series departures — Nexus manages seat inventory centrallyPOOL
Disconnected techMultiple tools per office — no single source of product or bookingsNexus platform + Zoho CRM — single source of truth for all ORN Connect product and bookingsCENTRALISE
Net position: Consolidating ORN Holidays and Skyjet overheads under a single ORN Connect P&L — combined with European office budget reallocation — funds the Nexus reservations team (Head of Reservations + 2 agents) at no new net cost. From Phase 2, ORN Connect inbound commission income exceeds the reservations team cost.
Booking Flow

End to end — one brand throughout

Because ORN Connect is a single entity, every step in the booking journey carries the same brand. No handoffs between separate companies — one team, one system (Nexus), one operator.

Flow A — Inbound trade agent booking
1

Agent enquiry

Trade agent contacts ORN Connect via Nexus portal, email or phone

2

Quote via Nexus

Res team prices using ORN Connect centrally contracted rates on Nexus

3

Confirmed

Booking confirmed through Nexus. Inbound commission margin captured on P&L

4

Transport allocated

ORN Connect transport team allocates coach or transfer from own fleet

5

Operate & earn

ORN Connect operates. All margin — contracting, operations, handling — on one P&L

Flow B — TripFactory / group office selling Europe inbound
1

Office sells

TripFactory or AU/USA/ME office sells UK & Europe inbound via their channel

2

Nexus request

Booking hits ORN Connect via Nexus — rates, availability, group configuration

3

Transaction fee

ORN Connect applies transaction fee to the group office for using contracted rates

4

ORN Connect operates

Hotels, coaches, guides confirmed and operated by ORN Connect. QC gate with Clinton Thomas

5

Income captured

Transaction fee + departure % — both on ORN Connect P&L. Single entity reporting

Roadmap

Four phases — one entity from day one

ORN Connect launches as a single brand from Phase 1. The Nexus reservations team, the transport division (previously Skyjet), and the contracting function all operate under one name from the start. Outbound is only added in Phase 4 once inbound is profitable.

Phase 1 — Launch

Brand, team, structure

  • Formally establish ORN Connect as the single UK & European DMC entity
  • Transition Skyjet brand to ORN Connect Transport Division
  • Appoint Head of Reservations — first and most critical hire
  • Configure Nexus platform for ORN Connect UK reservations
  • Notify all group offices — single contact is now ORN Connect
  • Confirm Incoming Europe partnership with Eileen Corrales
  • Consolidate all EU contracting budgets into ORN Connect UK P&L
Phase 2 — Inbound Open

Nexus live, tours operating

  • ORN Connect Nexus reservations open — agent portal live
  • First ORN Connect series departures — UK & European escorted tours
  • ORN Cruise inbound product on-sale via ORN Connect Nexus team
  • Hire Senior Res Agent and Res Agent — 3 people minimum
  • Transaction fee invoicing live with all group offices
  • Global product hub live — all offices on ORN Connect contracted rates
Phase 3 — Scale

Build on inbound

  • Zoho CRM live — full ORN Connect pipeline and booking reporting
  • Expand Nexus reservations team — target 4–6 agents
  • Quarterly group income reporting across all income streams
  • Expand ORN Connect tour programme — additional itineraries
  • Fleet capacity review — ORN Connect Transport Division Year 2 planning
  • Cost audit vs pre-consolidation (ORN Holidays + Skyjet) baseline
Phase 4 — Outbound

UK & European outbound

  • Extend ORN Connect to operate outbound FIT and GIT from UK and Europe
  • Nexus platform extended for outbound fulfilment
  • TripFactory B2C primary outbound distribution channel
  • MICE programme launched as managed group service
  • Long-haul partnerships (Macau, Cathay Pacific) integrated
ORN Connect Team

One team. One entity.

All roles sit under ORN Connect. Commercial contracting, transport (previously Skyjet), and Nexus reservations all report to Ajay Kawa as CEO UK & Europe. One P&L. No separate entities.

Connecting the World to Europe

One brand. Every destination. Every office. Every traveller.

Brand Vision

ORN Connect — what the brand stands for

ORN Connect is the connective tissue of the ORN Global Group. It connects every global office selling outbound to Europe — to the product, the transport, the hotels, and the experiences. It connects trade agents to live inventory via Nexus. It connects passengers to UK and European destinations. And it connects the group's commercial ambition to a single, scalable, expertly-run DMC operation based in London.

"ORN Connect is where every journey to Europe begins — for our clients, our agents, and our passengers."

Ajay Kawa · CEO UK & Europe

DESTINATION MANAGEMENT COMPANY 17 HANOVER SQUARE · LONDON
About ORN Connect

The UK & European DMC for the ORN Global Group

ORN Connect is a Destination Management Company headquartered at 17 Hanover Square, London. We manage all contracting, reservations, touring, transfers, and cruise product for the ORN Global Group. Previously operating as ORN Holidays (commercial) and Skyjet (transport), now unified under one brand. Powered by Nexus, our reservations team handles everything from first enquiry to on-ground delivery.

Sales & Operations Workflow

UK manages all sales. End to end.

ORN Connect UK takes on full sales and operations responsibility for inbound enquiries from all global offices — US, Australia, Middle East, and all trade agents. UK handles the full quote cycle, supplier pricing, confirmation, and pre-departure management. Payments are managed through the Australia office in Phase 1, transitioning to full UK end-to-end management in Phase 2.

Phase 1 — Quote & Sales Workflow
Step 1
Enquiry In
US / AU / ME office or trade agent sends enquiry to ORN Connect UK via Nexus or email
Step 2
UK Prices
UK team obtains all supplier prices — hotels (Ken Newton), transport (ORN Connect fleet), ground services (Incoming Europe)
Step 3
Quote Back
UK sends full quote to the originating office — detailed itinerary, net rates, inclusions, T&Cs
Step 4
Booking Confirmed
Office confirms booking. UK raises confirmation on Nexus, books all components with suppliers
Step 5
Payment — AU
Phase 1: All payments processed via Australia office. UK invoices AU; AU collects from originating office and pays suppliers
Step 6
Pre-departure
UK manages all pre-departure documentation, vouchers, and T-7 QC gate (Clinton Thomas)
Phase 1 payment flow: ORN Connect UK invoices the Australia office. Australia collects payment from the originating group office (US/ME/trade) and settles supplier invoices. This keeps the payment infrastructure on the established AU entity while UK builds its own financial setup.
Pre-Departure Reconfirmation Schedule

All bookings — regardless of lead time — follow the ORN Connect pre-departure gate schedule. Long advance bookings require additional T-45 reconfirmation to lock in all supplier commitments before the departure window opens.

T-45

Advance Reconfirmation

All long-lead bookings reconfirmed with every supplier. Hotel allotments, coach allocation, and ground services locked in. Any amendments resolved at this gate.

Long-lead bookings only
T-30

Full Confirmation Gate

All bookings reconfirmed. Rooming lists issued to hotels. Coach schedule confirmed with ORN Connect transport division. Final itinerary locked.

All bookings
T-14

Documentation Gate

Passenger manifests finalised. Pre-departure documentation and vouchers issued to agents and passengers. Any dietary or special requests confirmed with hotels.

All bookings
T-7

QC Sign-Off Gate

Clinton Thomas QC sign-off. Coach allocation confirmed. Final passenger count vs manifest. No departure proceeds without T-7 QC clearance.

Clinton Thomas · All GIT
Phase 2 — UK End-to-End (including Payments)

UK Collects Payments

ORN Connect UK invoices all global offices and trade agents directly. UK CFO manages all incoming payments, supplier settlements, and P&L reporting. No routing via Australia office.

Phase 2 · UK CFO hire required

Nexus Booking System

All bookings, quotes, and confirmations fully managed through Nexus. Automated confirmation flows, invoice generation, and pre-departure pack distribution — no manual workarounds.

Phase 2 · Nexus fully configured

Full P&L Visibility

UK P&L captures all revenue and costs — transaction fees, inbound commission, operated departure %, supplier payments — in one ORN Connect entity. Monthly reporting to Group CFO.

Phase 2 · UK CFO + Zoho CRM
Who Does What — UK Sales & Ops Team

Sales & Quoting — Nexus Res Team

All inbound sales enquiries and quote requests from US, AU, ME offices and trade agents are managed by the ORN Connect Nexus reservations team at 17 Hanover Square. Head of Reservations manages the queue and ensures turnaround within agreed SLAs.

Head of Res · Senior Agent · Res Agent

Operations — Transport & Ground

Once booked, ORN Connect transport division (previously Skyjet / Adonis Iliopoulos) manages all coach allocation, driver briefing, and transfer scheduling. Incoming Europe (Eileen Corrales) manages in-country ground services. Clinton Thomas owns the pre-departure QC gates.

Adonis Iliopoulos · Clinton Thomas · Eileen Corrales
Air Arabia UK Office

ORN Connect as Air Arabia's UK Representative

Air Arabia — the Middle East and North Africa's largest low-cost carrier, headquartered in Sharjah, UAE — is launching its first direct flights between Sharjah (SHJ) and London from 4 July 2026. ORN Connect will act as Air Arabia's UK office, managing their UK ground operation and providing a white label booking engine for the UK market — an extension of the same white label model ORN already operates for Air Arabia in the UAE.

The opportunity: Air Arabia operates 200+ routes from Sharjah to 170+ destinations across the Middle East, Indian Subcontinent, Central Asia, and Europe. The new London service opens a direct UK-UAE connection — and gives ORN Connect a significant anchor partnership with one of the region's fastest-growing carriers. AED 278 million net profit reported in Q1 2026.
4 Jul
London launch date 2026
SHJ
Sharjah hub
170+
Destinations globally
200+
Routes operated
White
Label model (existing UAE)
What ORN Connect Does as Air Arabia UK Office

White Label Booking Engine — UK

ORN Connect provides and manages a white label booking engine for Air Arabia's UK market — enabling UK consumers and travel agents to book Air Arabia flights, ancillaries, and holiday packages under the Air Arabia brand. This extends the existing white label model already operating in the UAE.

White label · UK market · Extension of UAE model

UK Office — Ground Representation

ORN Connect acts as Air Arabia's on-the-ground UK office — managing UK-based trade agent relationships, handling UK consumer and B2B enquiries, providing local market expertise, and supporting Air Arabia's UK commercial objectives from 17 Hanover Square.

UK representation · Trade agents · B2B & B2C

UK-Sharjah Connection — Inbound

The London–Sharjah route opens a direct inbound channel for ORN Connect. Middle East travellers flying Air Arabia to London land at one of ORN Connect's key inbound markets — UK and European tours, hotel product, and ground services can be pre-sold as add-on packages at point of Air Arabia booking.

Inbound ME market · ORN Connect product

Holiday Package Integration

ORN Connect's contracted UK and European hotel portfolio (27+ properties, Ken Newton) and transport fleet (96+ vehicles) can be packaged with Air Arabia flights — creating Air Arabia holiday packages for the UK market. Flight + hotel + transfer, all bookable via the white label engine.

Flight + hotel + transfer · Packaged product
About Air Arabia

The airline

Air Arabia is the Middle East and North Africa's first and largest low-cost carrier. Listed on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange, headquartered in Sharjah, UAE. Operates from three hubs: Sharjah (SHJ), Casablanca (CMN), and Alexandria (HBE). AirRewards loyalty programme with 10% cashback on all purchases.

Network & scale

170+ destinations across the Middle East, Indian Subcontinent, Central Asia, Africa, and Europe. 200+ routes. Expanding European footprint — daily flights to Rome from Sharjah launched February 2026. New London route launching 4 July 2026 is a major European milestone.

Financial performance

AED 278 million net profit in Q1 2026. 30% dividend distribution approved at 2026 AGM. One of the region's most profitable low-cost carriers. Strong balance sheet supports ongoing route expansion — including new long-haul European connections.

Implementation — ORN Connect UK Office for Air Arabia

Phase 1 — Launch (by 4 July 2026)

White label booking engine live for UK market. ORN Connect team briefed as UK office. 17 Hanover Square designated as UK contact address. Initial trade agent outreach to UK-based agents selling UAE and Middle East routes.

4 July 2026 target · Launch with London route

Phase 2 — Package & Grow

ORN Connect packages Air Arabia flights with UK and European ground product (hotels, transfers, tours). Holiday packages bookable via the white label engine. UK-Sharjah inbound travellers offered ORN Connect UK and European tour add-ons at point of booking.

Package product · Inbound add-ons · ME market
Strategic fit: Air Arabia's London launch on 4 July 2026 is perfectly timed with ORN Connect's own UK DMC launch. The white label model extends an existing commercial relationship into the UK. Middle East passengers arriving on Air Arabia flights to London are ORN Connect's core inbound customer — making this one of the most natural and commercially aligned partnerships in the business model.
